title: habenella garberi
intro: Habenella garberi, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snakebites and other poisonous bites, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an antidote for certain toxins. Habenella garberi is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
